title: Savory Kenyan Chili Peppers Stir-fry
description: A salty and crispy kid-friendly vegetarian dish featuring savory Kenyan chili peppers with pickle juice and a tadka (seasoning blend). This recipe is perfect for quick weekday meals.

ingredients.name: 1 cup pickled green papaya (or cucumber), drained and chopped
1 tablespoon tamarind paste or concentrated tomato sauce
ingredients.notes: Can use store-bought if not available

instructions: Heat the oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the red onion and garlic. Sauté for about 3 minutes until softened.
Add the tomatoes and cook for another 2-3 minutes, allowing them to break down slightly.
Stir in the pickled green papaya (or cucumber), tamarind paste, ground turmeric, water, and a pinch of salt. Mix well.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce heat to low and let it cook for about 10 minutes until most of the liquid has evaporated and you have a thick sauce.
Add the shredded coconut to the wok and stir-fry for another 5-7 minutes, until the coconut is lightly toasted and golden.
Stir in salt to taste. Serve hot with fresh coriander on top as garnish.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
